meta_title: For Founders: Git with AI to Solve no portfolio (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to Git with AI and overcome no portfolio. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Git with AI to Solve No Portfolio (Checklist)
content.seo_description: Stop worrying about your lack of portfolio. Use this expert checklist to leverage Git, Antigravity, and Claude Code to build revenue-generating SaaS MVPs fast.
content.answer_block: The 'no portfolio' trap is a myth; founders are judged by shipped products, not history. By using Git as your single source of truth and AI agents like Claude Code for rapid implementation, you can turn ideas into functional, monetizable SaaS MVPs in days, effectively out-shipping veteran developers.
content.intro:
The cold reality of the SaaS market is that nobody cares about your resume. Investors and customers don't want to see a history of what you've done; they want to see what you've built today. The 'no portfolio' anxiety is a barrier you create for yourself, while the market simply waits for a solution that solves their pain.

We are entering the era of the AI-augmented founder. By integrating Git with autonomous agents like Antigravity and Claude Code, you collapse the development lifecycle from months to hours. This isn't just about writing code; it's about building a public repository of value that proves your competence through execution rather than documentation.

steps.title: Define the Value-First MVP
steps.description: Identify one specific pain point to solve. Don't build features; build a solution that generates revenue.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a CTO. Analyze this business idea: [Insert Idea]. Identify the single most critical feature that provides immediate value and draft a technical requirements document.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Generate a project structure for a SaaS MVP focusing on [Goal] using Next.js and Tailwind.'

steps.title: Initialize the Git Repository
steps.description: Establish your version control hygiene early. A clean, commit-heavy history is your best proof of work.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a professional Git workflow with best practices for a solo founder, including branch protection and commit message standards.
steps.claude_code_command: git init && claude run 'Configure .gitignore and setup initial commit with a README explaining the project vision.'

steps.title: Automated Feature Scaffolding
steps.description: Use AI to write the boilerplate so you can focus on business logic and user experience.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold the core authentication and database schema for a SaaS application using Supabase and Prisma.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'Create user signup/login flow using Supabase Auth and link it to the landing page.'

steps.title: Iterative Logic Injection
steps.description: Use Claude Code to implement complex logic while you review the architecture.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the current codebase and suggest an optimization for the API route to handle higher concurrency.
steps.claude_code_command: claude fix 'The current subscription logic has a bug in the webhook handler. Diagnose and patch it.'

steps.title: Public Proof of Work
steps.description: Push your code to GitHub. A public repo with consistent commits is the ultimate portfolio.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a compelling README.md that highlights the problem, the solution, and the tech stack for potential investors.
steps.claude_code_command: git commit -m 'feat: implement core value proposition' && git push origin main

pro_tips: Treat your commits like a diary; meaningful commit messages are more important than the code itself for showing growth.
Use GitHub Issues to track your product roadmap; it turns your repo into a project management tool that signals professionalism.
Always build with a 'deploy-first' mindset; if it's not live, it doesn't exist in the eyes of your customers.
Leverage AI to document your code as you write it; well-documented code is a massive signal of seniority.
Don't aim for perfection; aim for 'shippable' so you can gather user feedback, which is the only metric that truly matters.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is live, focus on acquiring your first 10 paying customers. Use the feedback loop to iterate your code via the same Git/AI workflow established here.
content.conversion_block: Stop waiting for permission to be a founder. Your portfolio is built through the lines of code you push today, not the projects you dream about tomorrow. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
